Nobody talks about geographic arbitrage for App Store apps.

US market: crowded, expensive to acquire users, high LTV.
Germany: less competition, strong purchasing power, ignored by most.
Japan: loyal users, premium pricing accepted, almost no indie competition.
Brazil: fastest

There's a big problem with how people think about multi-agent systems.

I'm working with a company that designed their multi-agent system like a DAG, which most people do:

Agent A → Agent B → Agent C → Agent B → Agent D...

A flowchart like this works fine for linear tasks.

Huge context windows are the biggest lie in AI.

Honestly, I haven't seen any benefits to scaling past 1M tokens. The more data you show the model, the dumber they get, so larger windows are pointless.

Databases are far from dead.

Hot take within the vibe-coding community, but you can't build a reliable agentic memory system using files alone.

The filesystem is a great interface for agents, but for complex, distributed, production applications, databases win hands down.

I

Here is the funny part:

I've seen several teams already putting a ton of time "hardening" their filesystem implementation to support concurrency and improve search results.

Congratulations. You are just rebuilding a database!

Conclusions:

• Use files when building prototypes

Product 1: Inputr

A Chrome extension that fixes any image upload box on the web.
Detects what the box wants dimensions, format, file size limit then lets you upload + crop, create from scratch, or draw something at the exact constraints. All without leaving the page.

Free. Open
